volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Ghost (Section 5.1.1097e - Section 5.2.1166b)

sections.label: Section 5.1.1097e
sections.original: Scies .
sections.translation: You shall hear ; why , because I especially want this , for them to be taking refuge there . Do let them ; so much the more easily shall I get him fined before the judge .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1100
sections.original: Quod agas id agas . quid tu porro serere vis negotium ?
sections.translation: What you intend to do , do it . Why do you wish to sow further strife ? You don ’ t know how ticklish a thing it is to go to law .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1102
sections.original: Surgedum huc igitur . consulere quiddam est quod tecum volo .
sections.translation: Just get up , ( beckoning ) this way ; it ’ s , then , to ask your advice upon something that I want you .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1103
sections.original: Sic tamen hinc consilium dedero . nimio plus sapio sedens .
sections.translation: Still , as I am , I ’ ll give my advice from this spot : my wits are much sharper when I ’ m sittings [*] . Besides , advice is given with higher sanction from holy places [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1105
sections.original: Surge , ne nugare . aspicedum contra me .
sections.translation: Get up ; don ’ t be trifling . Just look me in the face .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1105b
sections.original: Aspexi .
sections.translation: I am looking .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1105c
sections.original: Vides ?
sections.translation: Do you see me ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1106
sections.original: Video . huc si quis intercedat tertius , pereat fame .
sections.translation: I do see — that if any third person were to step in here , he would die of hunger .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1107
sections.original: Quidum ?
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1107b
sections.original: Quia nil illi quaesti sit . mali hercle ambo sumus .
sections.translation: Because he would get no profit ; for , upon my faith , we are both artful ones .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1108
sections.original: Perii .
sections.translation: I ’ m undone !

sections.label: Section 5.1.1108b
sections.original: Quid tibi est ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter with you ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1108c
sections.original: Dedisti verba .
sections.translation: You have deceived me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1108d
sections.original: Qui tandem ?
sections.translation: How so , pray ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1109b
sections.original: Vide sis , satine recte : num mucci fluont ?
sections.translation: Consider , please , if it wasn ’ t well done ; is your nose running still ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1110
sections.original: Immo etiam cerebrum quoque omne é capite emunxti meo .
sections.translation: Aye , all my brains besides have you been wiping out of my head as well . For all your villanies I have discovered from their very roots ; and not from the roots , indeed , i ’ faith , but even from beneath the very roots .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1113
sections.original: Numquam edepol hodie hinc , si vivo , invitus desistam tibi .
sections.translation: Never this day , by my troth , will you have planned all this without being punished .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1114
sections.original: Iam iubebo ignem et sarmenta , carnifex , circumdari .
sections.translation: I shall at once , you villain , order fire and faggots [*] to be placed around you .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1115
sections.original: Ne faxis , nam elixus esse quam assus soleo suavior .
sections.translation: Don ’ t do it ; for it ’ s my way to be sweeter boiled than roasted .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1116
sections.original: Exempla edepol faciam ego in te .
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I ’ ll make an example of you .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1116b
sections.original: Quia placeo , exemplum expetis ?
sections.translation: Because I please you , you select me for an example .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1117
sections.original: Loquere : quoius modi reliqui , quom hinc abibam , filium ?
sections.translation: Say now : what kind of a person did I leave my son , when I went away from here ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1118
sections.original: Cum pedibus , manibus , cum digitis , auribus , oculis , labris .
sections.translation: One with feet and hands , with fingers , ears , eyes , and lips .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1119
sections.original: Aliud te rogo .
sections.translation: I asked you something else than that .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1119b
sections.original: Aliud ergo nunc tibi respondeo .
sections.translation: For that reason I now answer you something else .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1120
sections.original: sed eccum tui gnati sodalem video húc incedere
sections.translation: But look , I see Callidamates , the friend of your son , coming this way . Deal with me in his presence , if you want anything

sections.label: Section 5.2.1122
sections.original: Vbi somno sepelivi omnem atque édormivi crapulam ,
sections.translation: When I had buried all drowsiness [*] , and slept off the debauch , Philolaches told me that his father had arrived here from abroad ; in what a way too his servant had imposed upon the man on his arrival ;

sections.label: Section 5.2.1125
sections.original: ait se metuere in conspe ctum sui patris pr ocedere .
sections.translation: he said that he was afraid to come into his presence . Now of our company I am deputed sole ambassador , to obtain peace from his father . And look , most opportunely here he is . ( Accosting THEUROPIDES .) I wish you health , Theuropides , and am glad that you ’ ve got back safe from abroad . You must dine here with us to-day . Do so .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1130
sections.original: Callidamates , dei te ament . de cena facio gratiam .
sections.translation: Callidamates , may the Gods bless you . For your dinner I offer you my thanks .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1131
sections.original: Quin venis ?
sections.translation: Will you come then ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1131b
sections.original: Promitte : ego ibo pro te , si tibi non libet .
sections.translation: Do promise him ; I ’ ll go for you , if you don ’ t like .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1132
sections.original: Verbero , etiam inrides ?
sections.translation: Whip-scoundrel , laughing at me still ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1132b
sections.original: Quian me pro te ire ad cenam autumo ?
sections.translation: What , because I say that I ’ ll go to dinner for you ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1133
sections.original: Non enim ibis . ego ferare faxo , ut meruisti , in crucem .
sections.translation: But you shan ’ t go . I ’ ll have you carried to the cross , as you deserve .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1134
sections.original: Age mitte ista ac te ad me ad cenam dic venturum .
sections.translation: Come , let this pass , and say that I shall go to the dinner .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1134b
sections.original: Quid taces ?
sections.translation: Why are you silent ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1135
sections.original: Sed tu , istuc quid confugisti in aram ?
sections.translation: But why , you greatest of simpletons , have you taken refuge at the altar ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1135b
sections.original: Inscitissimus
sections.translation: He frightened me on his arrival . ( To THEUROPIDES .) Say now , what I have done amiss . Look , now there ’ s an umpire for us both ; come , discuss the matter .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1138
sections.original: Filium corrupisse aio te meum .
sections.translation: I say that you have corrupted my son .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1138b
sections.original: Ausculta modo .
sections.translation: Only listen . I confess that he has done amiss ; that he has given freedom to his mistress ; that in your absence

sections.label: Section 5.2.1140
sections.original: faenore argentum sumpsisse ; id esse absumptum praedico .
sections.translation: he has borrowed money at interest ; that , I admit , is squandered away . Has he done anything different to what sons of the noblest families do ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1142
sections.original: Hercle mihi tecum cavendum est , nimis qui es orator catus .
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I must be on my guard with you ; you are too clever a pleader .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1143
sections.original: Sine me dum istuc iudicare . surge , ego isti adsedero .
sections.translation: Just let me be umpire in this matter . ( TO TRANIO .) Get up ; I ’ ll seat myself there .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1144
sections.original: Maxime , accipito hanc tute ad te litem .
sections.translation: By all means : take the management of this dispute to yourself .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1144b
sections.original: Enim istic captio est .
sections.translation: Why , this is surely a trick .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1145
sections.original: fac ego ne metuam ⋯ ut tu meam timeas vicem .
sections.translation: Make me , then , not to be in a fright , and yourself to be in a fright in my stead .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1146
sections.original: Iam minoris omnia alia fa cio , prae quam quibus modis
sections.translation: I consider now everything of trifling consequence , compared with the way in which he has fooled me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1147b
sections.original: Bene hercle factum , et factum gaudeo :
sections.translation: I ’ faith , ’ twas cleverly done , and I rejoice that it was done . Those who have white heads ought at that age to be wiser .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1149
sections.original: Quid ego nunc faciam ?
sections.translation: What am I now to do

sections.label: Section 5.2.1149b
sections.original: Si amicus Diphilo aut Philemoni es ,
sections.translation: if my friend Demipho or Philonides —

sections.label: Section 5.2.1150
sections.original: dicito eis , quo pacto tuos te servos ludificaverit :
sections.translation: Tell them in what way your servant made a fool of you . You would be affording most capital plots for Comedies .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1152
sections.original: Tace parumper , sine vicissim me loqui , ausculta .
sections.translation: Hold your tongue awhile ; let me speak in my turn .— Listen .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1152b
sections.original: Licet .
sections.translation: By all means .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1153
sections.original: Omnium primum sodalem me esse scis gnato tuo .
sections.translation: In the first place of all then , you know that I am the companion of your son ;

sections.label: Section 5.2.1154_1155
sections.original: is adiit me , nam eum prodire pudet in conspectum tuom
sections.translation: he has gone to my house , for he is ashamed to come into your presence , because he knows that you are aware what he has done . Now , I beseech you , do pardon his simplicity and youthfulness . He is your son ; you know that this age is wont to play such pranks ; whatever he has done , he has done in company with me . We have acted wrong :

sections.label: Section 5.2.1160
sections.original: faenus , sortem sumptumque omnem , quí amica empta est , omnia
sections.translation: the interest , principal , and all the sum at which the mistress was purchased , all of it we will find , and will contribute together , at our own cost , not yours .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1162
sections.original: Non potuit venire orator magis ad me impetrabilis
sections.translation: No mediator could have come to me more able to influence me than yourself . I am neither angry with him [*] , nor do I blame him for anything : nay more , in my presence , wench on , drink , do what you please .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1165
sections.original: si hoc pudet , fecisse sumptum , supplicí habeo satis .
sections.translation: If he ’ s ashamed of this , that he has been extravagant , I have sufficient satisfaction .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1166
sections.original: Dispudet .
sections.translation: I ’ m quite ashamed myself

sections.label: Section 5.2.1166b
sections.original: Post istam veniam quid me fiet nunciam ?
sections.translation: He grants pardon thus far ; now then , what is to become of me ?
